commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f1bac4903405a860fee49253630ae5a070491176)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an embedded-attempt cleanup helper that logs, rather than rejects, `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` when `params.abortSignal` is already aborted.

PR surface: Source +21. Total +21 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch defect from source inspection: Gateway timeout aborts use the same aborted signal that the PR treats as user-initiated. The original Stop-button UX still lacks live reproduction proof in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer/contributor follow-up for real behavior proof and a product-safe abort classification; automation cannot supply the external PR's Stop-button proof.

target.title: fix(agents): suppress EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError on user-ini…
target.body:
## Summary

When a user presses the Stop button during agent thinking, the session fence mechanism detects session file modification and throws `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`. This error is injected into the conversation context, polluting the agent's next turn with noise.

This PR identifies user-initiated aborts (via `params.abortSignal?.aborted`) and suppresses the takeover error from conversation context while still logging it for debugging.

### Key Changes

- Add `shouldSuppressTakeoverErrorOnUserAbort()` helper function
- Check `userInitiatedAbort` before injecting error into context
- Log warning instead of `Promise.reject` for user abort case
- Session fence mechanism still effective (safety boundary preserved)

### Issue Reference

Fixes #88903

## Verification

### Behavior addressed

Suppresses `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` from conversation context when user presses Stop button, preventing error pollution in agent's next turn.

### Real environment tested

- Local tests: `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts` (42 tests passed)
- Code changes: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` (+22 lines)

### Exact steps or command run after this patch

1. Checkout branch: `fix/suppress-session-takeover-error-on-abort-88903`
2. Run tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts --run`
3. Verify lint: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`

### Evidence after fix

- Test suite: 42 tests passed (2.18s)
- No new lint errors introduced
- Session fence mechanism still active (log.warn still triggers)

### Observed result after fix

User-initiated abort (Stop button) → takeover detected → **log.warn only** (error suppressed from context)
Non-user abort (timeout, etc.) → takeover detected → error injected normally (as system failure)

### What was not tested

- Live user interaction (Stop button press in real UI)
- Integration with full agent runner workflow
- Multi-agent concurrent access scenarios

---

**Related issues**: #87966, #88369, #88457, #88471, #88653 (distinct from this fix - those address false positives, this addresses UX impact of legitimate user abort)
target.author: tanshanshan
